ThF(4) evaporated on two diodes. Layer thickness

of Th. Mean energy loss in ThF(4) layer 2.9+-0.25 MeV.
Two semiconductor diodes were mounted on a
common axis 36 mm apart from a vacuum chamber.
Detectors were made from 500 OMEGA*cm silicon, with
gold of 40 ug/cm2. Operated at 55 V voltage.

fresh when peak-to-valley ratio of Cf-252 pulse height

Lower detection threshold for pile-up rejection
Mass and energy of fragments were calculated from mass
and momentum conservation low. Evaporation of neutrons
from primary fragments was taken into account using
universal curve of Terrell ( see REL-REF).
